user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You have TWO separate things you want done in this chat, but you bring them up one at a time like a real person would:

  (1) FIRST: you're traveling for a few weeks, so you want to PAUSE your subscription -- not cancel it, just pause it so you can resume later.
  (2) THEN: you also want your DEFAULT payment method switched to your Mastercard (the one ending in 5555), which is already saved on your account. You are NOT adding a new card -- you just want to make the Mastercard you already have your default.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and ONLY the first request -- pausing the subscription (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- can you pause my subscription while I'm traveling?"). Do NOT mention the payment-method change yet. Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal subscription or payment ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent look things up.
- Make it clear you want to PAUSE, not cancel. If the agent seems about to cancel, correct them.
- Once the agent has confirmed the pause and asks whether there's anything else you need, THEN bring up the second request: switching your default card to the Mastercard ending in 5555. (If the agent instead tries to wrap up without asking, go ahead and raise it yourself.)
- Refer to the card the way a person would: "my Mastercard, the one ending in 5555." It is ALREADY on file -- if the agent asks for a new card number or offers to add a new card, decline; you only want to switch to the card you already have.
- Once the agent has confirmed BOTH the pause and the new default card, and there's nothing else, you're satisfied and done.
